summary: Sugaree shines, Lazy Lightning>Supplication peaks, Cosmic Charlie glows
blurb: The show delivers a standout Sugaree and a soaring Lazy Lightning > Supplication sequence that lifts the second set. Jerry’s guitar work is inventive throughout, while Phil provides an “insane” bass performance on several numbers. Cosmic Charlie and Scarlet Begonias add extra sparkle.
review:
September 25 1976 at the Capital Centre captured the Dead in a rare moment of tight‑but‑exploratory magic. The first set rolls through classic crowd‑pleasers – Bertha, New Minglewood Blues and a crisp, soaring Sugaree where Jerry’s lead lines were described as flawless and inventive. Phil’s bass turns “insane” on that same track, and his slide work shines on Scarlet Begonias later in the night. The second set is the show’s high‑point: a Lazy Lightning → Supplication stretch that builds into a rapturous climax, followed by a rare Cosmic Charlie that several listeners called a highlight of the entire tour. The band rides the momentum through Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo, a tight Dancin’ In The Street segue and a double Saint Stephen/Not Fade Away jam that keeps the groove rolling to a triumphant Sugar Magnolia finish.

Audio-wise the best source is the crisp soundboard (gd76-09-25.sbd.aj.246.sbefail.shnf). Though it runs slightly slow and needs a modest pitch correction, its clarity lets you hear every nuance – from Keith’s tasteful piano interplay on Cassidy to the “greatest Phil bomb” that punctuates the set. Audience recordings exist but are hampered by reverb and mid‑range loss, making the SBD the clear choice for anyone wanting to experience this show at its full color.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the performance – Sugaree, Lazy Lightning>Supplication, and Cosmic Charlie receive consistent positive language ("flawless", "insane", "stellar"). No recording reports sustained negative criticism of playing; mixed sentiments refer mainly to audio quality, not musical execution.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard capture with clear mix; despite slight speed issue it presents the full set, highlighting Sugaree, Lazy Lightning > Supplication and Cosmic Charlie.

key_highlights: Lazy Lightning → Supplication sequence shines as the second‑set centerpiece.
Jerry’s flawless guitar on Sugaree and Minglewood Blues.
Phil delivers an “insane” bass performance and a standout solo (the "greatest Phil bomb").
Rare Cosmic Charlie appears, adding extra excitement to the night.

band_performance.Jerry: Flawless, inventive and at the top of his game – standout guitar on Sugaree, Minglewood Blues and throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Insane bass work, especially on Sugaree and Scarlet Begonias; delivers the “greatest Phil bomb” during Mississippi Half‑Step.
